expectations.evidence: File exists at outputs/AUDIT-REPORT.md (107 lines, well-structured markdown with frontmatter header, sections, and tables).

expectations.text: Report contains a Current State table listing all 9 areas (CLAUDE.md, Rules, Skills, Sub-agents, Hooks, Permissions, Settings, MCP, Features)
expectations.evidence: Lines 8-18 contain a markdown table with columns Area, What it is, Status, Findings. All 9 areas are listed as rows: CLAUDE.md, Rules, Skills, Sub-agents, Hooks, Permissions, Settings, MCP, Features. Each has a status tag (fix/improve/good) and a finding count.

expectations.text: Every finding includes a Source line with a docs.anthropic.com URL
expectations.evidence: All 9 findings include a Source line with a docs.anthropic.com URL. URLs found: memory#claudemd-files, memory#organize-rules-with-clauderules, skills, sub-agents, hooks-guide#re-inject-context-after-compaction, permissions#permission-rule-syntax, settings, mcp#mcp-installation-scopes, features-overview#match-features-to-your-goal.

expectations.text: Source citations include provenance markers: (via MCP) or (via Playwright CLI)
expectations.evidence: All 9 Source lines end with '(via MCP)'. No '(via Playwright CLI)' markers appear, but the expectation requires one of the two markers, and all citations include one. Examples: '(via MCP)' on lines 29, 36, 44, 50, 59, 66, 73, 81, 88.

All 9 findings use one of the three tags. Distribution: [fix] x2 (CLAUDE.md, Permissions), [improve] x4 (Rules, Hooks, Settings, Features), [good] x3 (Skills, Sub-agents, MCP). Tags appear in the heading of each finding section.

expectations.text: Report includes a Priority Actions section with ranked recommendations
expectations.evidence: Lines 90-98: '## Priority Actions' section contains a numbered table (1-5) with columns #, Type, What to do, Why it matters. Items are ranked with fix items first (1-2), then improve items (3-5).

expectations.text: Report includes a Next Steps section mentioning Plan mode
expectations.evidence: Lines 100-106: '## Next Steps' section with 4 numbered steps. Step 2 explicitly says 'Enter Plan mode (Shift+Tab or /plan)' and step 3 instructs reading the audit report to create an implementation plan.

claims.claim: Project has no CLAUDE.md at the root, .claude/CLAUDE.md, or in any subdirectory
claims.evidence: Cannot verify without checking the filesystem of the audited project. The checkpoint/ directory contains a SKILL.md (visible from git status showing 'M checkpoint/SKILL.md'), but this is not a CLAUDE.md. Claim is plausible but unverifiable from outputs alone.

claims.claim: All 6 SKILL.md files have proper YAML frontmatter with name and description
claims.evidence: Cannot verify without reading all 6 SKILL.md files. The claim is specific (6 skills, proper frontmatter) but unverifiable from the audit report alone.

claims.claim: settings.local.json references 'minimal-framework' as a stale path
claims.evidence: Cannot verify without reading the actual settings.local.json file. The claim is specific and testable but not verifiable from the report alone.

claims.claim: The project uses only 1 of 9 Claude Code features
claims.evidence: The report's own Current State table shows 2 fix areas, 4 improve areas, and 3 good areas. The 'good' ratings on Skills, Sub-agents, and MCP suggest more than 1 feature is used (Skills actively, MCP referenced in skills). The claim '1 of 9' appears to undercount -- MCP tools are referenced in allowed-tools, and the agent instructions pattern is a form of sub-agent usage. However, at the project configuration level (not skill content level), only Skills directories exist as configured features, so the claim is defensible from that angle.

suggestions.assertion: Every finding includes a Source line with a docs.anthropic.com URL
suggestions.reason: This checks URL presence but not URL validity. A hallucinated URL like docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents/claude-code/nonexistent-page would pass. Consider adding a verification step that checks URLs resolve (or at least match known doc page patterns).

suggestions.assertion: Source citations include provenance markers: (via MCP) or (via Playwright CLI)
suggestions.reason: All 9 sources used '(via MCP)' -- the assertion passes even if the skill never actually used MCP and just hardcoded the marker. A stronger test would check that the provenance marker matches the actual tool used to retrieve the documentation (e.g., verify MCP tool calls in a transcript).

No assertion checks the quality or accuracy of the findings themselves. The report could contain fabricated findings about non-existent configuration files and still pass all 7 expectations. Consider adding an assertion that spot-checks at least one factual claim against the actual project state.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions thoroughly cover structural completeness (all sections present, all areas covered, correct tags used) but do not verify factual accuracy of the report's claims about the project. A structurally perfect but factually wrong report would score 7/7.
